page: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. Local storage is bundled directly with the instance, consisting of 7600 GB of high-speed NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. The server achieves a top-tier Passmark CPU score of 88,092.76, driven by strong results in extended instructions and encryption tests. Multi-core stress-ng performance is rated as strong at 124,962.53, while single-core performance remains in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 335.25 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 129.91 GB/sec for write operations. The platform also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in specialized tasks such as ray tracing and image processing.

From a resource density perspective, the c6id.metal offers high compute and local storage density. The inclusion of 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides a cost-efficient alternative to provisioning external block storage for high-throughput workloads. However, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for memory-bound applications requiring massive datasets to be held entirely in RAM.

This instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require direct access to physical hardware and high-speed local storage. Suitable applications include high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, batch processing, and electronic design automation (EDA). It is also well-suited for multi-threaded compilation and web serving workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs from dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 RAM and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 50 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio optimizes the server for compute-heavy tasks, while the integrated high-capacity NVMe storage provides a cost-efficient alternative to external block storage. This server is designed for workloads such as high-performance computing, large-scale databases, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier compute and encryption performance for demanding workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core performance.

7600 GB of integrated high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
256 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
